patch.value: 2026-05-31 late latest-main ecosystem dogfood and review closure

Follow-up latest-main manual audit after PR #89 merge: verify full pm CLI, SDK, package ecosystem, external packages, agent/token-efficient UX, docs, CI/CD, security, telemetry/Sentry, and performance in isolated temporary projects; document any remaining issues without duplicating the closed pm-kd9n audit.

Latest main is dogfooded in isolated temporary projects, external pm packages are verified, specialist sub-agent findings are recorded, actionable issues are fixed or tracked, local gates pass, changes are committed/pushed to a descriptive PR, review feedback is handled, checks are green, and PR is merged back to main when clean.

value.author: docs-ci-security-late-review-20260531
value.text: Start: docs/CI/security/observability read-only specialist audit after latest main/PR #89. Scope: README/docs progressive disclosure, AGENTS alignment, workflows/free-service assumptions, secret hygiene, Sentry/telemetry gates, release scripts, stale/duplicated docs. Constraints: no file edits, no item create/claim/release; private host details redacted/kept out of tracked comments.

value.author: code-quality-late-review-20260531
value.text: Code-quality/performance specialist final: no file edits performed. Commands run: git status/rev-parse; gh pr view 89; pnpm typecheck; pnpm quality:static; pnpm quality:docs-skills; node scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs --json; node scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs --json --max-lines 1800 --max-lines-tests 2500 --max-complexity 80 --max-duplicate-chunks 0; pnpm audit --prod; pnpm outdated --format list; rg/nl/wc/find/sha256sum dependency/type/duplication probes. Findings: (1) medium: static gate is green but thresholds are too loose for the stated low-complexity goal: defaults allow max_src_lines=3400, max_test_lines=7000, max_complexity=260 (scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs:302-340). A stricter audit exposed complexity hotspots: src/cli/register-mutation.ts:71 registerMutationCommands complexity 195, src/cli/commands/update.ts:1036 runUpdate complexity 193, src/cli/commands/create.ts:1177 runCreate complexity 152, src/cli/commands/update.ts:1489 mutate complexity 131, src/cli/commands/extension.ts:880 runExtension complexity 127, src/cli/commands/config.ts:974 runConfig complexity 105, src/mcp/server.ts:499 runAction complexity 105. Recommendation: split command-registration groups and mutation phases, then ratchet gate thresholds. (2) medium: search hydrates full bodies for all cached documents before metadata/exact filters: loadDocuments reads/re-parses bodies at src/cli/commands/search.ts:1131-1196 and runSearch calls it before filtering at src/cli/commands/search.ts:1279-1287. Recommendation: metadata-first filter, then hydrate bodies only for keyword/include-linked paths; semantic-only queries can build the id map from metadata. (3) medium-low: long SDK/extension/CLI hubs remain maintenance and review-risk: src/cli/main.ts 2385 lines, src/core/extensions/loader.ts 2105, src/cli/commands/completion.ts 2006, src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts 1948. Existing gate allows this, but these are central extensibility surfaces. Recommendation: keep compatibility re-exports but move more tables/renderers/dispatchers behind focused modules and test each module directly. (4) low: type-safety can be ratcheted: tsconfig.json:8 is strict but tsconfig.json:10 uses skipLibCheck and no noUncheckedIndexedAccess/exactOptionalPropertyTypes. External package runtime still uses any[] casts for imported collections at packages/pm-todos/extensions/todos/runtime.ts:340-346. Recommendation: add package-local validators/narrowers and trial stricter compiler flags in CI as non-blocking first. (5) low: dependency/security status is acceptable but footprint should be watched: pnpm audit --prod found no known vulnerabilities; pnpm outdated reports commander 14.0.3 => 15.0.0 and tsx 4.22.3 => 4.22.4; @sentry/node is a production dependency and resolves to about 6.5M in pnpm store while used via dynamic import in src/core/sentry/instrument.ts:216. Recommendation: schedule commander-major compatibility review and consider whether Sentry can remain lazy/optional without increasing install footprint. Duplicate probes found no byte-identical duplicated docs, skills, or test files.

value.author: agent-ux-late-review-20260531
value.text: Final agent UX/contracts read-only audit: Findings ordered by severity. Medium: read-only/list-query flag typo normalization still silently rewrites near-miss flags before Commander validation. In isolated temp PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH, pm list-open --limt 1 and --lmit 1 both exited 0 and applied limit=1; --fiields 1 and --fieldsx 1 were normalized to --fields and failed on value validation, with recovery showing canonical --fields instead of the original typo. This is non-mutating, but for agent-safe contracts it can hide bad tool calls and produce plausible but unintended context. Relevant code: src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts resolveCanonicalFlag/normalizeLongOptionToken normalizes Levenshtein typo matches for all command families; src/cli/main.ts only blocks mutating typo rewrites via enforceExplicitRetryForMutatingFlagTypos. Low/residual: dist/cli-bundle/main.js was intermittently missing during probes while other local coverage/test processes were running, causing transient ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND from dist/cli.js; later repeated package command help probes passed, so I treat this as concurrent local build artifact churn, not a confirmed runtime product bug. Already fixed by pm-kd9n: mutating typo recovery now refuses auto-correct and gives explicit suggested retry; pm create --acceptance and create/update --descrption/--titel did not mutate temp state. pm get default help advertises standard projection; list outputs remain compact TOON by default; optional package command absence gives install hints; after pm install all --project, calendar/guide/templates/todos/test-runs/search-advanced/governance commands exposed JSON help; package doctor summary returned ok with 0 warnings; pm health --check-only --summary --json on repo returned ok with warning_count=0. Commands run included gh pr view 89, pm show pm-kd9n --json, pm contracts --flags-only/--runtime-only/--availability-only --json, pm create/get/list/package catalog/package doctor in isolated temp state, typo probes, package install all, and package command --help --json probes. No file edits, no item create/claim/release, no reverts.

value.author: sdk-package-late-review-20260531
value.text: SDK/package ecosystem specialist final: no intentional file edits, no pm item create/claim/release, no reverts. Commands/source reads: gh pr view 89; pm get pm-kd9n/pm-etxf --json; pm contracts --command package/install/upgrade --flags-only --json; pm contracts --runtime-only --availability-only --json; pm package catalog/explore/doctor --json; npm run dogfood:package-first twice; node --import tsx src/cli.ts package help/catalog/doctor/contracts/upgrade probes; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts tests/unit/package-runtime-options.spec.ts tests/unit/package-import-adapters.spec.ts tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts tests/unit/builtin-extension-entrypoints.spec.ts; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/extension-startup.integration.spec.ts tests/integration/mcp-dynamic-package-actions.spec.ts; node scripts/check-secrets.mjs; node scripts/release/docs-skills-gate.mjs --json; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate; pm health --check-only --summary --json; rg/wc/sed/jq source inspections. Results: package doctor deep ok with 0 warnings; runtime contracts expose package lifecycle actions and installed pm-changelog action; catalog projects 8 bundled packages; upgrade --packages-only --project --dry-run plans pm-changelog with failed=0; package-first temp dogfood passed on rerun with 87 commands including init --with-packages, bundled install aliases/wildcards/all, local package root, npm:local package root, package scaffold install/invoke, package commands, runtime contract availability, and upgrade; focused SDK/package tests passed 118/118 plus package integration tests 12/12; secrets/audit/docs gate/health passed after build completed. Findings ordered by severity: (1) Low actionable, not source/runtime break, not fully covered by pm-kd9n: scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs invokes dist/cli.js directly and can fail transiently in a shared checkout while another process rebuilds dist/cli-bundle; first run hit ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for a bundle chunk/main during concurrent local build churn, immediate rerun passed. Consider making the harness detect missing/incomplete dist and return deterministic guidance to run/wait for pnpm build, or prefer a per-run built artifact path in concurrent specialist audits. (2) Informational/already covered by pm-kd9n: public SDK exports, package manifest resource model, scaffold shape, package doctor collision/remediation contracts, optional package action runtime contracts, package install aliases/wildcards/local/npm-local sources, and upgrade package planning are covered by pm-kd9n and passed current probes. (3) Informational: first-party bundled TS sources still deep-import repo src for development, but shipped JS runtimes are self-contained or load public dist SDK runtime through PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT/local runtime loaders; docs explicitly restrict PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT/deep imports to first-party packages. No new duplicate pm item recommended from this lane.

value.author: docs-ci-security-late-review-20260531
value.text: Final docs/CI/security/observability specialist audit (read-only): reviewed README/docs progressive disclosure, AGENTS alignment, GitHub workflows, release scripts, tracked secret hygiene, Sentry/telemetry gate behavior, changelog check, ignored telemetry runbooks, and PR #89 status. Finding 1 (medium, CI/agent-concurrency): local release gate coverage failed in this shared checkout with 76 test failures rooted in dist/cli.js importing missing dist/cli-bundle/main.js during sandbox init; a focused rerun passed after the bundle reappeared. scripts/bundle-cli.mjs deletes dist/cli-bundle before rebuilding, scripts/run-tests.mjs can skip build and reuse shared dist, and scripts/release/run-gates.mjs runs coverage with PM_RUN_TESTS_SKIP_BUILD=1 after a prior build. In a multi-agent checkout this can produce false red gates while another build rewrites dist. Recommend serializing build/test gates per checkout, using isolated worktrees for subagents, or making run-tests copy/build the CLI bundle into an immutable temp artifact before Vitest. Finding 2 (low, release-doc precision): docs/RELEASING.md says external Sentry checks run when a token is configured in the auto-release policy, while release.yml now requires SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN for the release Sentry issue threshold gate and skips only sourcemap upload without SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN. The setup section is accurate; tighten the policy wording to avoid maintainers thinking release reliability is optional. Positive evidence: PR #89 is merged and reported green checks; pnpm quality:docs-skills passed; pnpm security:scan passed with no tracked credential-like secrets; pnpm changelog:pm:check reported CHANGELOG unchanged with 931 closed items; pm health --check-only --summary --json passed with 0 warnings; Sentry/telemetry gate passed in aggregate with 0 critical/high unresolved issues and telemetry best-effort ok; scripts/prod telemetry files containing private operational details are ignored and have 0 tracked files. Validation caveat: pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift --json had ok=true with warning validate_resolution_missing_fields:29 and history drift ok. I did not edit source/docs/workflows, create items, claim/release, or revert others; the changelog check refreshed a managed-extension timestamp and I restored that one-line self-introduced diff to keep the worktree read-only aside from this item comment.

value.note: clarify required Sentry release reliability gate

value.author: codex-late-audit-20260531
value.text: Evidence: late latest-main audit used four specialist sub-agents for SDK/package, agent UX/contracts, code quality/performance, and docs/CI/security. Verified bundled dogfood (87 commands), semantic dogfood (89 commands with qwen3-embedding:0.6b), external pm-changelog isolated install/doctor/contracts/generate, Sentry/telemetry gate (critical 0, high 0, error rate 1.22%, missing error rows 0), telemetry stack health (queue depth 0, 1 consumer, 11482 events last 24h), GitHub open issues/PRs empty, dependabot alerts fixed-only, local audit/security clean. Actionable fixes: query flag typos now require explicit retry instead of silently narrowing read context; release docs clarify required Sentry reliability gate. Note: concurrent dist rebuilds caused transient ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND during parallel gates; serial coverage and release:gates passed.

patch.value: Work fully landed on main. The 2026-05-31 late-main audit's actionable output (query-flag-typo explicit-retry guard + mixed-case command classification) reached main via PR #91 (41d366e0) and was confirmed/carried by PR #94 (merge da6a38ef, 2026-06-01) — during the PR #94 merge the canonical main versions of src/cli/main.ts, help-runtime.spec.ts, and this task's tracker were taken. Specialist sub-agent findings were recorded as comments; remaining actionable items are tracked (pm-kcba release-publish gap). All CI checks green on PR #94; merged back to main clean. Acceptance met.
